items.title: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type leading to Privilege Escalation
items.summary: Adobe Commerce is affected by an Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type vulnerability that could result in arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user, potentially leading to privilege escalation. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 9.6.

items.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output leading to Arbitrary Code Execution
items.summary: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source are affected by an improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability that could lead to arbitrary code execution. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 9.1.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization leading to Security Feature Bypass
items.summary: Adobe Commerce is affected by an Incorrect Authorization vulnerability that could result in a Security feature bypass, allowing an attacker to gain unauthorized read and write access. Exploitation does not require user interaction.

items.title: Form Field Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
items.summary: A vulnerability was found in Adobe Commerce (version 2.4.9 and earlier) related to a form field cross-site scripting issue. The attack may be performed remotely.

items.title: Critical Remote Code Execution in Amasty Extension
items.summary: Amasty issued a massive security release for 25 extensions, with one critical vulnerability that can lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, opening the door to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered an unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. The flaw is rated 9.8 (critical).

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution
items.summary: This critical vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution. Adobe released a security update to address this and other issues.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution
items.summary: A critical vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to arbitrary code execution. This was addressed in the APSB26-05 security update.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization (Auth Bypass)
items.summary: An Incorrect Authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access. It has a CVSS 3.1 base score of 7.5.

items.title: Arbitrary File System Write
items.summary: This vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary file system write. It was resolved as part of the APSB26-05 security update.

items.title: Application Denial-of-Service
items.summary: A vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could result in an application denial-of-service. This issue was addressed in the APSB26-05 security bulletin.

items.title: Security Feature Bypass
items.summary: This vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to a security feature bypass. Adobe released a security update to resolve this and other issues.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload via REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered the PolyShell vulnerability, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files using a polyglot technique to bypass validation. This was fixed in Magento 2.4.9.
